decisions.decision: Keep the account service-index contract in the existing bare-path style and add `/v1/accounts/{id}/exposure` as a sibling GET-only route whose `route_request(...)` branch mirrors `/risk` and delegates directly to `get_account_exposure(account_id)`.
alternatives_rejected: Switch `routes.accounts` to method-qualified strings such as `GET /v1/accounts/{id}/exposure`.
Fold exposure reads into `/v1/accounts/{id}/risk` or another combined account endpoint.
Introduce a special exposure adapter instead of routing straight to `get_account_exposure(...)`.

decisions.decision: Backfill the missing resolved-market exposure cleanup seam in `backend/server.py` and invoke it from `resolve_market_command(...)` alongside `settle_market_account_risk(...)`, because this checkout still lacks that wiring.
alternatives_rejected: Assume another branch already added cleanup and limit this step to route registration only.
Leave stale resolved-market exposure rows in `ACCOUNT_EXPOSURE` until a later follow-up.
Inline one-off cleanup logic inside `resolve_market_command(...)` without a reusable helper.

decisions.decision: Define resolution cleanup over the live exposure projection only: remove every position row whose `marketId` matches the resolved market across all outcomes, refresh surviving accounts' `updatedAt`, and prune accounts whose final live exposure disappears so `/v1/accounts/{id}/exposure` preserves its projection-local `404 account_not_found` behavior.
alternatives_rejected: Keep resolved-market positions visible as archival exposure history.
Delete exposure rows but keep empty account documents around for read-time filtering.
Expand cleanup into unrelated historical `ORDERS`, `COMMANDS`, or `EVENTS` mutation.

decisions.decision: Keep this step scoped to the server-index, router, and resolve-path wiring named in the task; if the new cleanup helper returns structured data, retain it on the in-memory resolution result as an internal seam, but do not widen the accepted-resolution HTTP payload unless the current implementation already requires it.
alternatives_rejected: Expand this patch into a broader resolve-response contract change.
Drop any cleanup result entirely and force later work to rescan `ACCOUNT_EXPOSURE`.

steps.task: Patch `service_index_payload()` and the account branch in `route_request(...)` so the service index advertises `/v1/accounts/{id}/exposure` and the router accepts `GET /v1/accounts/{id}/exposure` with the same `405 method_not_allowed` guard pattern already used for `/v1/accounts/{id}/risk`.

Add a small market-scoped exposure cleanup helper in `backend/server.py` near the exposure projection utilities that scans `ACCOUNT_EXPOSURE`, removes all rows for the resolved `marketId`, updates surviving account timestamps, and prunes empty exposure accounts.

Update `resolve_market_command(...)` to call the new exposure cleanup helper alongside `settle_market_account_risk(...)`, keeping the cleanup limited to live exposure state and storing the helper result only if needed as an internal resolution seam.
